notes: Nakahigashi et al. Comprehensive identification of TIS by TetRP. E. coli K-12 BW25113. ~150 new starts within 60 nt of annotated site; ~360 novel intergenic TIS.
Meydan et al. Retapamulin-Assisted Ribosome Profiling Reveals the Alternative Bacterial Proteome. Primary TIS in 86% of expressed genes (BW25113); 239 internal TIS candidates.
Saito et al. Translation initiation in E. coli occurs at correct sites in absence of mRNA-rRNA base-pairing. Confirms ~54% of E. coli genes carry SD motifs; SD affects efficiency not site selection.

Zhu et al. Snapshot of translation in M. tuberculosis during exponential growth and nutrient starvation. 3569 genes = 88.5% of annotated translatome. Starvation: 3032 genes; 16% leaderless, 17% SD, 19% UNSD.

Cortes et al. Genome-wide mapping of TSS defines an extensive leaderless transcriptome in M. tuberculosis. 26% of genes with primary TSS have TSS overlapping start codon = leaderless (no 5' UTR / SD).

Kohl et al. Extended SD motifs govern translation initiation in S. aureus (Nat Commun Feb 2026). S. aureus uses extended start-codon-proximal SD interactions; creates specificity against phylogenetically distant bacteria. Several native S. aureus TIS not decoded correctly by E. coli ribosomes.

n_SD: ~78% of genes carry SD motif (genomic analysis)
notes: Lalanne et al. Evolutionary convergence of pathway-specific enzyme expression stoichiometry. B. subtilis Ribo-seq; dataset used as reference for TIS analysis. Genomic SD prevalence: ~78% (Wen et al. 1995, Nakagawa 2010 estimates).
Bhatt et al. Translation in B. subtilis is spatially and temporally coordinated during sporulation. Comprehensive Ribo-seq dataset across sporulation stages.

Schrader et al. The coding and noncoding architecture of the C. crescentus genome (PLoS Genet). ALPHAPROTEOBACTERIA = 4th bacterial phylum for RBS. Multi-method TIS: start codons corrected for 12.8% of CDS, 94 new CDS, 2726 TSS via 5'-RACE. Genome NA1000 = CP001340. PMID verify at fetch time.

Gelsinger et al. Ribosome profiling in archaea reveals leaderless translation, novel TIS, ribosome pausing (NAR 48(10):5201). ARCHAEA = bacteria->archaea DOMAIN-TRANSFER axis; >70% leaderless (2nd leaderless-rich organism after MTB). Accession + PMID/DOI verify at fetch time from paper data-availability.
